codeReview.summary: The feature correctly centralizes homepage prewarm target selection behind the same resolved main-window identity helper used by bootstrap rendering, but it regresses the existing no-prewarm contract by issuing a backend prewarm call during primary-main bootstrap and leaves contradictory test coverage in place.

issues.description: `useAppBootstrap` now calls `crab.prewarmWindow` whenever `getHomepagePrewarmTarget(nextWindow)` resolves. This reintroduces a homepage-triggered prewarm side effect during bootstrap, contradicting the feature tests and the surrounding contract that startup correctness must not depend on automatic prewarm timing. The same feature still ships `shouldRequestWindowPrewarm` assertions expecting no hidden-window preparation, so the implementation and test contract diverge.

The existing `shouldRequestWindowPrewarm` tests still assert that no hidden-window preparation is ever requested, even though the feature now intentionally returns a homepage prewarm target for the true primary main window. The suite passes only because `shouldRequestWindowPrewarm` is no longer exercised by bootstrap, leaving stale coverage that can mislead future changes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skill says to write failing tests first and manually verify the Tauri window surface, but this feature only added logic-unit coverage and no desktop-surface verification. The handoff marks the procedure as followed even though interactive verification did not happen, and the mission guidance already defers that manual pass.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: window-controls-worker/SKILL.md steps 3 and 8 require failing tests first and desktop verification; worker transcript shows only Bun/typecheck/lint commands and the handoff has interactiveChecks: [] with skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo now contains two overlapping homepage-prewarm decision surfaces: `getHomepagePrewarmTarget` drives real bootstrap behavior while `shouldRequestWindowPrewarm` and its tests still encode the older never-prewarm rule. The mission docs mention authoritative identity resolution but do not spell out which exported helper is the single supported API for homepage prewarm decisions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/flow/bootstrap/logic.ts:65-97 exports both getHomepagePrewarmTarget and shouldRequestWindowPrewarm; tests/bootstrap.logic.test.ts:226-318 still centers assertions on shouldRequestWindowPrewarm even though src/flow/bootstrap/index.ts:40-42 now uses getHomepagePrewarmTarget directly.

summary: Review failed: homepage routing alignment is mostly correct, but the feature reintroduces a bootstrap prewarm side effect that conflicts with the feature’s own no-prewarm assertions and leaves stale test coverage around the legacy helper.
